zh.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coffyn I是Sir Richard Coffin的兒子，於1168年出生在英國戴文阿爾文頓。他至少有1個兒子。他去世在法國奧維普-德-o帕倫特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coffyn I是Sir Richard Coffin的儿子，于1168年出生在英国戴文阿爾文頓。他至少有1个儿子。他去世在法国奧維普-德-o帕倫特。

nameMeanings.name: Coffyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southwestern England, of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Middle English cofin, coffin, Old French cof(f)in (from Late Latin cophinus, Greek kophinos) ‘container, basket; coffer, chest (for keeping treasures, documents, armour, etc.)’. Early bearers of this as a hereditary surname were of knightly rank. Old French cofin was synonymous with coffer, and it may be that Cofin was used to denote a keeper of the (royal) coffer, attested in Anglo-Latin cofferarius. Compare Coffer . The modern English word coffin is a specialized development of this term, not attested until the 16th century.
History: Tristram Coffin came from Brixham, Devon, to Haverhill, MA, before 1647. An important line of his descendants is associated with Nantucket and Martha's Vineyard.

variants.name: Coffer
meanings: English: metonymic occupational name from Middle English cofre, coffre, coffer ‘box, chest’ (from Old French cof(f)rehere), used to denote a box or coffer maker (Old French coffrier), perhaps also for a treasurer.
Probably also an Americanized form of German Kaufer or Kauffer.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coffyn I was born in 1168, in Alwington, Devon, England as the son of Sir Richard Coffin. He had at least 1 son. He died in Parent, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coffyn I est né en 1168, à Alwington, Devon, Angleterre, fils de Sir Richard Coffin. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé à Parent, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, France.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coffyn I wurde 1168 in Alwington, Devon, England, als Sohn von Sir Richard Coffin geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in Parent, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coffyn I è nato il nel 1168 nel Alwington, Devon, England come figlio di Sir Richard Coffin. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel Parent, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coffyn Iは1168年にイギリスデボンアルウィントンでSir Richard Coffinの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼はフランスアウバーグンプウイ-デ-ドoメパーエントで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coffyn I(은)는 1168년 영국 데폰 알윙턴에서 Sir Richard Coffin의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 프랑스 오버그니 푸이-데-드o 파렌트에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coffyn I nasceu em 1168, em Alwington, Devon, Inglaterra filho de Sir Richard Coffin. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em Parent, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coffyn I родился в 1168 г. в местности алвингтон, девон, Англия. Его отца звали Sir Richard Coffin. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в местности парент, пуи-де-дoм, аувиргн, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coffyn I nació en 1168, en Alwington, Devon, Inglaterra como hijo de Sir Richard Coffin. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en Parent, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, Francia.
